patch.value: Audited history-repair (re-anchor) command + clear legacy history drift so pm health is ok

pm health and pm validate --check-history-drift have returned ok:false for months because 31 legacy history streams carry stale hash chains (31 chain mismatches + 15 current-item hash mismatches) left over from old hand-edits, hierarchy repairs, and the 2026-05 redaction one-off scrub. pm-xk39 shipped the redaction command (which re-anchors a chain ONLY when a redaction match occurs); nothing re-anchors a cleanly-drifted stream, so the drift never clears. This is a real agent-UX failure: every agent that runs `pm health` sees ok:false and cannot distinguish benign legacy drift from real corruption.

Fix: add a first-class, audited `pm history-repair` mutation (the missing peer to history-redact) that deterministically replays a stream from empty, re-anchors every before/after hash, leniently normalizes legacy patch ops that no longer strict-apply (replace->add on first-write paths; skip unresolvable array-shape ops), appends a reconciliation entry so the latest after_hash equals the current on-disk item document, and records an op=history_repair audit marker. Then run it across the 31 drifted streams so pm health/validate return ok with zero drift. Consolidate the duplicated replay/patch helpers shared by history/restore/history-redact into src/core/history/replay.ts (addresses pm-pjs5).

author: claude-code-agent
message: Implementing audited history-repair command + clearing legacy drift; consolidating replay helpers (pm-pjs5).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implementation + drift cleanup complete (2026-05-22). (1) Shipped audited `pm history-repair <id>`: replays a stream from empty, recomputes every before/after hash, recovers legacy patch ops that no longer strictly apply (replace->add on first-write paths; skips unresolvable array-shape ops), reconciles the latest hash with the on-disk item, and appends an op=history_repair audit marker. Never modifies item content; safe no-op on clean streams; --dry-run/--author/--message/--force with the same ownership+lock governance as history-redact. (2) Consolidated replay/patch mechanics into src/core/history/replay.ts; history/restore/history-redact now share it (pm-pjs5) with per-command error wrappers preserved. (3) Edge case discovered+fixed: a legacy stream whose first entry is a partial update (pm-lty) replays to a non-canonicalizable doc; replayHash now falls back to a deterministic structural hash for such states (full docs unaffected). (4) Ran on all 31 drifted real streams: pm health now ok=true (was ok=false for months), validate --check-history-drift drifted=0. git diff shows only history JSONL changed; no item content touched. (5) check-secrets initially failed because the privacy sub-agent had quoted literal secret-pattern example strings into its pm-xk39 evidence comment; scrubbed via audited history-redact -> check-secrets passes.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Agents (including sub-agents) must never quote literal secret-pattern example strings (private-key markers, /home/<user>/ paths, real IPs) into pm comments/notes: scripts/check-secrets.mjs scans tracked pm history+item files and will fail the release gate on those literals. Describe patterns abstractly instead. Use audited history-redact to scrub if it happens.
